title: oxycoccus palustris subsp. microphyllus
intro: Oxycoccus palustris subsp. microphyllus, also known as small-leaved cranberry or dwarf cranberry, is a subspecies of the Oxycoccus palustris plant, which is a type of low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the boreal forests of Europe. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and organic acids, which are believed to possess medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oxycoccus palustris subsp. microphyllus include its potential to help manage urinary tract infections, improve immune function, and support cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Oxycoccus palustris subsp. microphyllus can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
